cases.title: Ruff v. Nashville Presbytery
cases.synopsis: The SJC sustained a complaint that Nashville Presbytery failed to conduct an adequate BCO 31-2 investigation of allegations against TE George Grant and erred in finding no strong presumption of guilt when its own Shepherding Committee's letter acknowledged serious, credible concerns. The matter was remanded to Nashville Presbytery for further proceedings consistent with the opinion.

cases.title: McNeil v. Chesapeake Presbytery
cases.synopsis: The SJC ruled that McNeil's appeal was judicially out of order because he had not submitted to a regular trial, having refused to enter a plea or appear before Chesapeake Presbytery's Judicial Commission, and that his subsequent censure of suspension from the sacraments eliminated his standing to file further complaints against post-censure actions of the court.

cases.title: Sartorious et al. v. Siouxlands Presbytery

parties.complainant_or_appellant: TE Art Sartorious, TE Brian Carpenter, TE Wes White

topics: fundamentals of the system of doctrine
cases.synopsis: The SJC denied a complaint that Siouxlands Presbytery erred in finding no strong presumption of guilt in TE Joshua Moon after an investigation into allegations that his theological views on Federal Vision topics were heterodox, holding that deference to the presbytery was required absent clear error in the record. The case generated a concurring opinion offering procedural guidance on BCO 31-2 investigations versus BCO 32-2 charges, and a dissenting opinion by RE Haigler arguing the record showed unambiguous heterodoxy warranting a finding of strong presumption of guilt.

cases.title: Lyons v. Western Carolina Presbytery
topics: BCO 34-2 scandalous charges on slight grounds
cases.synopsis: The SJC denied a complaint by Kirk Lyons challenging Western Carolina Presbytery's dismissal of charges he filed against TE Craig Bulkeley, finding no clear error in the presbytery's determination that grounds existed under BCO 31-8, 34-2, and 32-20 to decline the charges, though the SJC noted the presbytery erred (non-prejudicially) in denying Lyons standing, since filing BCO 32-2 charges brought him under the court's jurisdiction.

cases.title: Gulfstream Presbytery — Citation re: Review of Presbytery Records
cases.synopsis: Gulfstream Presbytery was cited and appeared before the SJC regarding exceptions of substance found in its records; the SJC accepted the presbytery's response as acceptable, pending certification that the response was approved by the presbytery.

cases.title: Korean Central Presbytery — Citation re: Review of Presbytery Records
cases.synopsis: Korean Central Presbytery was cited and appeared before the SJC regarding exceptions of substance found in its records; the SJC accepted the presbytery's response as acceptable, pending certification that the response was approved by the presbytery.

cases.title: Korean Northwest Presbytery — Citation re: Review of Presbytery Records
cases.synopsis: Korean Northwest Presbytery was cited and appeared before the SJC regarding both exceptions of substance and failure to submit minutes; the SJC accepted the presbytery's response on the substance exceptions and noted the minutes had been submitted.

cases.title: Korean Southeastern Presbytery — Citation re: Review of Presbytery Records
cases.synopsis: Korean Southeastern Presbytery was cited for failure to submit minutes; the SJC noted the minutes had been submitted and forwarded them to the Committee on Review of Presbytery Records.

cases.title: Korean Southwest Presbytery — Citation re: Review of Presbytery Records
cases.synopsis: Korean Southwest Presbytery was cited for failure to submit minutes; the SJC noted the minutes had been submitted and forwarded them to the Committee on Review of Presbytery Records.

cases.title: Pacific Presbytery — Citation re: Review of Presbytery Records
cases.synopsis: Pacific Presbytery was cited and appeared before the SJC regarding exceptions of substance found in its records; the SJC accepted the presbytery's response as acceptable, pending certification that the response was approved by the presbytery.
